Most comprehensive resource I've seen." Jerry Liu CEO of LlamaIndex Engineers from global leaders are learning with us Are you making these AI system design mistakes? Most AI projects fail in production. Not from lack of skill, but from predictable architectural choices. 01 Over-Engineered AI Systems Defaulting to "agent + tools" leads to unnecessary complexity, compounding failure points and debugging nightmares. 02 Discovering Requirements Late Unclear requirements cause cost blowups, tool sprawl, and untestable autonomy. 03 Reliability Risk AI systems fail in production through hallucinated outputs, edge case failures, and poor observability. What You'll Learn 01 A Process To Make Clean Design Decisions Instead of defaulting to agents + tools, you'll have a clear framework to make architectural decisions using constraints. 02 Start With Clear Requirements Learn the kickoff questions that force clarity upfront, so cost blowups, tool sprawl, and untestable autonomy don't show up after you've already built half the system. 03 Design For Reliability From Day 1 Practical rules for making agents production-safe dialing in a thin agent layer with validation loops, observability, and human-in-the-loop design. 04 Architect For Millions, NOT Your First Demo Learn to build the architecture for real user traffic, keeping your agents thin so the system is debuggable and observable in production. Conversion wins on cost, speed, retention, and context: your developers already hold the domain, systems, and codebase knowledge the AI systems will run on, while a new hire spends a year learning what your team already knows. Generic training stalls Courses divorced from real workflows don't stick BCG's 2025 work on the AI skills gap reaches the same conclusion we see in client engagements: upskilling holds when it is tied to the team's actual systems and priorities. Every format below is scoped against your baseline and approved use cases. Early conventions compound Your first trained cohort sets the standard The first group that learns to build, evaluate, and ship LLM systems defines how your organisation does it from then on. Train that group deliberately rather than letting habits form by accident. 02 The offer Three delivery routes, one curriculum direction Agentic development teaches people to direct and verify models in their day job; AI engineering adds the disciplines that put models inside products. Each route is independently scoped. None is a prerequisite for another, and we will tell you in the scoping call which one fits. A Cohort course access A live kickoff plus bulk access to the relevant Academy programme, giving a developer cohort one shared foundation. Anthropic 2026 · GitHub · MIT · Amazon Untrained use backfires METR found experienced developers 19% slower with AI on familiar code while believing they were faster. 66% of developers report frustration with “almost right” output and 45% say debugging AI code takes longer. METR 2025 · DORA 2025 The gap is trainable Experts drive roughly 12 agent actions and 3,200 words of output per prompt versus roughly 5 and 600 for novices.
